metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: bug (shell-escape persisted as a plain user message â†’ parser drives working with no settle, sticks); driver_capability: gap:keys

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: bug
driver_capability: gap:keys

## Reasoning

**agent (yes).** Gemini CLI has an interactive shell-escape: typing `!cmd` (or a bare `!` to toggle persistent shell mode) runs the command in a LOCAL shell via `ShellExecutionService.execute` with NO LLM round-trip â€” confirmed in the bundled source (`bundle/interactiveCli-OYDA765S.js`, `handleShellCommand`: it builds `callId = shell-<ts>`, calls `addItemToHistory({type:"user_shell",text:rawQuery})`, then `ShellExecutionService.execute(...)` directly â€” never `chat.sendMessage`). Verified live: a tmux `gemini -y` REPL with `!echo hello_shell_escape` ran the command, printed the output, and toggled into shell mode, all without a model call.

**daemon (BUG â€” verified live, supersedes my doc-only hypothesis).** I first hypothesized from the bundle that the shell-escape stays a UI-only `user_shell` HistoryItem and never reaches the persisted session JSONL (it is absent from the `recordHistory` chunks, which fire only on `chat.sendMessage`). The LIVE recording DISPROVED that: Gemini DOES persist the shell-escape to the session rollout (`~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/session-*.jsonl`) as an ORDINARY `type:"user"` text message whose content is `"I ran the following shell command:\n```sh\necho hello_shell_escape\n```\n\nThis produced the following result:\n```\nhello_shell_escape\n```"` â€” and NO `gemini` assistant message follows it (no LLM turn). 

Feed that line through `core/adapters/inbound/agents/geminicli/parser.go::parseUser`: it has no `functionResponse` part (so not `function_call_output`), `firstText` is non-empty and does not start with `<session_context>`, so the parser falls through to `ev.EventType = "user_message"` + `ev.ClearToolNames = true`. That drives **readyâ†’working**. Because Gemini emits NO terminal `gemini` text turn for a shell-escape, the parser's only settle path (parser.go:209 â€” a `gemini` message with non-empty content and zero toolCalls â†’ `turn_done`) NEVER fires. The session **STICKS in working** until the user later sends a real prompt that completes. This directly violates the scenario's invariant: 'state does not stick in working after the shell command exits.'

This is a `bug`, not `incapable`: a clean local trace exists (the persisted user line); the daemon mis-handles it by classifying a non-LLM shell-escape report as a real user turn. The correct fix mirrors claudecode's `isLocalCommandContent` skip-list (parser.go:216-225): recognize the `"I ran the following shell command:"` shell-escape preamble in `parseUser` and skip it (no `user_message`, no `ClearToolNames`), so state stays `ready` throughout â€” exactly the no-flicker behavior claudecode chose for `!ls`. Spec stays asserting the CORRECT arc (state stays ready, never sticks in working); `known_failing` is set so `record` files the issue and records the failure.

**driver (gap:keys).** The recipe must elicit the shell-escape WITHOUT desyncing the driver's turn accounting. `send_text` (driver-interactive.sh:218-227) unconditionally increments `EXPECTED_TURNS`, and `wait_turn` blocks until `turn_count()` (gemini messages with text + no toolCalls) reaches it. A shell-escape produces a `user` line but NO gemini turn, so a `send`-then-`wait_turn` recipe over-counts and times out. The reference claudecode recipe sidesteps this by sending the shell-escape via a `keys` step (`! e c h o ... Enter`), which does NOT bump `EXPECTED_TURNS`. The gemini-cli driver STUBS `keys` (`not_implemented keys`) and it is absent from `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"`, so recipe-lint flags it as a semantic gap. `record` must port the `keys` arm (raw `tmux send-keys` of `! e c h o Space h e l l o Enter`) before it can drive â€” pure tooling, not an observability limit.

## What record must do

Port the driver `keys` arm, then drive: keys-send the shell-escape, sleep (NO wait_turn â€” no gemini turn lands), send a normal prompt, wait_turn (proves the session is alive and a real turn drives workingâ†’ready). The recording will show the session entering working on the shell-escape and NOT settling â€” confirming the bug and feeding the filed issue.
caveats: DAEMON BUG confirmed by a live tmux recording (gemini 0.46.0): the shell-escape is persisted as a plain `type:"user"` text message ('I ran the following shell command: ...'), which parser.go classifies as `user_message` â†’ readyâ†’working with no settle, so the session sticks in working. Contrast claudecode, which wraps shell-escape in `<bash-input>/<bash-stdout>` and skips it. The fix is a parseUser preamble skip mirroring claudecode's isLocalCommandContent.

My initial doc-only read (shell-escape is a UI-only user_shell HistoryItem that never reaches the rollout, so daemon=full/no-transition) was WRONG and the live recording corrected it â€” recorded here so a future audit doesn't re-litigate the absence of `user_shell` from the recordHistory chunks (recordHistory only fires on chat.sendMessage; Gemini separately re-injects the shell result as a user message into the persisted history).

DRIVER GAP: the `keys` step type is stubbed (not in DRIVE_ELICITS); `record` ports it before driving. A bare `send "!echo hello"` works to TRIGGER shell mode (verified live) but desyncs EXPECTED_TURNS and breaks the trailing wait_turn, so the recipe uses `keys` like the claudecode reference.

RECORDING PREREQ: Gemini OAuth (oauth-personal) must be set up out-of-band; the Google One / unpaid tier stops serving on 2026-06-18, after which recordings on those tiers fail auth. The migration banner appears in-REPL today.

The first `!` also toggles persistent shell mode (the REPL shows 'shell mode enabled (esc to disable)'); the recipe sends Escape (or relies on the keys arm sending only one command) before the normal prompt so the alive-check prompt is not eaten as another shell command.

sources.note: parseUser: a user-role message with no functionResponse and non-empty firstText not prefixed <session_context> falls through to EventType `user_message` + ClearToolNames=true â†’ readyâ†’working. The only settle path is parser.go:209 (a `gemini` message with non-empty content + zero toolCalls â†’ turn_done). A shell-escape produces the former and never the latter â†’ state sticks in working. No skip-list for the shell-escape preamble exists (unlike claudecode).
Lines 216-225 isLocalCommandContent skip-list (<bash-input>/<bash-stdout>) â€” the analogous handling gemini-cli's parser LACKS. This is the shape of the fix the bug requires.

LIVE RECORDING (gemini 0.46.0, tmux): after `!echo hello_shell_escape` the rollout gains exactly one `type:"user"` line with content 'I ran the following shell command:
```sh
echo hello_shell_escape
```

This produced the following result:
```
hello_shell_escape
```' and NO following `gemini` message. A subsequent normal prompt produced a `gemini` text turn ('alive') that settled. This is the events-grade trace anchoring daemon=bug.

handleShellCommand: addItemToHistory({type:'user_shell',text:rawQuery}) then ShellExecutionService.execute(...) â€” local shell, no chat.sendMessage, no LLM round-trip. Confirms agent=yes and that the shell-escape is not a model turn.

DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep" (no keys); keys arm is `not_implemented keys`. send_text bumps EXPECTED_TURNS unconditionally and wait_turn blocks on gemini-turn count, so the shell-escape must be elicited via the (stubbed) keys step to avoid a turn-count desync â†’ driver=gap:keys.

preconditions: gemini CLI on PATH and authenticated via Google OAuth (oauth-personal), set up out-of-band
irrlichd is recording (dev daemon with --record, isolated IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_HOME + alt port so prod is untouched)
Driver runs `gemini -y --skip-trust` so no trust-folder gate blocks launch
The driver `keys` arm has been ported (record ports it before driving) so the shell-escape can be sent without bumping EXPECTED_TURNS
setup: Driver stages a fresh cwd and launches gemini -y under tmux
wait_turn resolves the rollout transcript created after launch and blocks on its gemini-turn count

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ready
Reply with exactly the word: alive

verify: events.jsonl shows the session entering ready after launch

EXPECTED (correct) behavior the spec asserts: during the ~6s window after the !echo shell-escape, NO state_transition to working fires (the parser must learn to skip the shell-escape preamble, like claudecode skips <bash-input>/<bash-stdout>)

KNOWN-FAILING today: the parser instead emits user_message â†’ readyâ†’working with no settle, so the session sticks in working during that window â€” this is the bug the recording demonstrates and the filed issue tracks
after the normal prompt, events.jsonl shows a workingâ†’ready settle (the terminal 'alive' gemini turn)
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to waiting for the session during the entire run
